market_source: Yahoo Finance ^GSPC and ^IRX via project data_manager.load_backtest_data

lag_policy.unemployment: FRED UNRATE monthly observations forward-filled to market sessions, then shifted 21 market sessions before use
lag_policy.spx_trend: SPX SMA20/SMA50 trend computed from end-of-day close and shifted one market session before use

unemployment_signal_definitions.SEVERE_UNEMPLOYMENT_TREND: UNRATE_RISING_6M and prior-session SPX below SMA50

scope_definitions.three_x_only: Only baseline 3x recovery exposure can be reduced.
scope_definitions.all_recovery: Baseline 2x and 3x recovery exposure can be reduced; cash and 1x base exposure are preserved.

UNRATE.lag: 21 market sessions after monthly observation date

ranking_policy: practical score weights max drawdown improvement, CAGR retention, Sharpe delta, and modestly penalizes leverage churn

paid_data_recommendation.reason: This specific unemployment momentum test is already covered by public, directly accessible FRED UNRATE. Paid data would matter more for point-in-time earnings revisions, forward EPS breadth, constituent-level breadth, layoff announcements, or real-time macro vintages than for the basic unemployment throttle.
potential_providers: ALFRED/FRED vintage data via St. Louis Fed for true real-time unemployment release vintages
FactSet or Refinitiv I/B/E/S for point-in-time earnings revision breadth
S&P Global/Compustat or Bloomberg for point-in-time index constituent and fundamental breadth
paid_data_recommendation.access_note: Direct access would require user-provided API credentials or a local data export for paid providers.
